name: Aloo Gobi
description: Classic dry curry of potatoes and cauliflower with turmeric and cumin.
steps: Dice potatoes and cut cauliflower into florets. Chop onion and tomato finely.
Heat oil, add cumin seeds and let splutter. Add onion and sauté 5-6 min until golden.
Add ginger-garlic paste and green chilies; cook 1 min. Add tomatoes, turmeric, coriander, chili powder, and salt; cook 5 min.
Add potato and cauliflower; toss to coat. Sprinkle 2 tbsp water, cover and cook on low 20-25 min, stirring occasionally.
Sprinkle garam masala and cook uncovered 2-3 min. Garnish with cilantro and serve.

name: Palak Paneer
description: Soft paneer cubes in a vibrant, creamy pureed spinach gravy.
steps: Blanch spinach in boiling water 2 min; transfer to ice water, drain and blend smooth.
Pan-fry paneer cubes in oil until golden on all sides; set aside.
Heat butter and oil; add cumin. Add onion and sauté until translucent. Add ginger-garlic paste and chili; cook 1 min.
Add tomatoes, turmeric, coriander, and salt. Cook until tomatoes break down, 5 min.
Pour in spinach puree, simmer 8-10 min. Add paneer and garam masala; simmer 5 min.
Stir in cream, adjust salt, and serve with naan or rice.

name: Baingan Bharta
description: Smoky mashed eggplant tempered with onion, tomato, and spices.
steps: Roast whole eggplant directly on a flame, turning, until charred all over. Cool, peel, and mash.
Heat mustard oil; add cumin and onion. Sauté until golden, 5-6 min.
Add ginger-garlic paste and chili; cook 1 min. Add tomatoes, turmeric, and salt; cook 5 min.
Add mashed eggplant; mix well and cook 8-10 min, stirring.
Sprinkle garam masala and coriander. Garnish with cilantro and serve.

name: Bhindi Masala
description: Crispy okra stir-fried with onion, tomato, and aromatic spices.
steps: Wash and dry okra thoroughly. Cut into 1-inch pieces.
Heat oil; fry okra on medium-high heat stirring often until no longer slimy, about 10 min. Remove and set aside.
In the same pan add onion and sauté 5 min. Add ginger-garlic and chili; cook 1 min.
Add tomatoes, turmeric, coriander, and salt. Cook until tomatoes break down.
Return okra to pan; toss gently. Cook 3-4 min. Sprinkle amchur and serve.

name: Chana Masala
description: Bold chickpea curry in a tangy tomato-onion gravy with whole spices.
steps: Soak chickpeas overnight; pressure cook until tender, about 4 whistles.
Heat oil; add cumin. Sauté onion until deep golden, 7-8 min.
Add ginger-garlic paste and chili; cook 1 min. Add tomatoes and all spices; cook until oil separates, 6-8 min.
Add chickpeas with 1 cup cooking water. Mash a few chickpeas to thicken gravy.
Simmer 15 min. Adjust salt, garnish with cilantro and serve.

name: Dal Tadka
description: Yellow lentils with a sizzling aromatic tempering of ghee, cumin, and dried red chili.
steps: Rinse yellow lentils thoroughly and soak 30 min. Pressure cook with 3 cups water for 3 whistles until soft.
Heat oil in a pan. Add cumin seeds, then onion; sauté until golden, about 5 min.
Add ginger-garlic paste, green chili, turmeric, and cook 1 min. Add tomatoes and cook 4 min.
Pour the cooked lentils into the masala. Stir well and simmer 10 min.
Make a tadka: heat ghee in a small pan, add dried red chili and cumin; pour over dal.
Adjust salt and consistency with water. Serve with steamed rice.

name: Aloo Matar
description: Tender potatoes and peas simmered in a light tomato-onion gravy.
steps: Prep potatoes and peas: wash, peel if needed, and cut into bite-sized pieces.
Heat oil in a pan over medium heat. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter for 30 seconds.
Add chopped onion and sauté 5-6 min until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and green chili; cook 1 min.
Add tomatoes, turmeric, coriander powder, and salt. Cook until tomatoes break down, about 4-5 min.
Add potatoes and peas. Stir to coat with masala. Cover and cook on low heat 15-20 min, stirring occasionally.
Sprinkle garam masala, adjust salt, garnish with cilantro and serve hot with rice or roti.

name: Malai Kofta
description: Deep-fried potato-paneer dumplings in a rich, creamy tomato gravy.
steps: Boil and mash potatoes; crumble paneer. Mix with salt and spices; shape into balls and deep-fry until golden.
Blend onion, tomato, and soaked cashews into a smooth paste.
Heat butter; cook the paste 10 min until oil separates. Add cardamom, cinnamon, and garam masala.
Add 1 cup water and simmer 5 min. Stir in cream.
Gently add koftas to the gravy just before serving to keep them soft.

name: Paneer Tikka Masala
description: Grilled paneer and bell peppers in a smoky, spiced tomato-cream sauce.
steps: Marinate paneer and bell pepper in yogurt, garam masala, chili powder, and salt for 30 min. Grill or pan-fry until charred.
Blend tomatoes and onion to a smooth puree. Heat butter and cook puree 10 min.
Add ginger-garlic paste, turmeric, garam masala; cook 3 min.
Add 0.5 cup water and simmer 8 min. Stir in cream.
Add grilled paneer and peppers; simmer 5 min and serve.

name: Mushroom Matar
description: Button mushrooms and peas cooked in a fragrant onion-tomato gravy.
steps: Prep mushrooms and peas: wash, peel if needed, and cut into bite-sized pieces.
Heat oil in a pan over medium heat. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter for 30 seconds.
Add chopped onion and sauté 5-6 min until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and green chili; cook 1 min.
Add tomatoes, turmeric, coriander powder, and salt. Cook until tomatoes break down, about 4-5 min.
Add mushrooms and peas. Stir to coat with masala. Cover and cook on low heat 15-20 min, stirring occasionally.
Sprinkle garam masala, adjust salt, garnish with cilantro and serve hot with rice or roti.

name: Mixed Vegetable Korma
description: Assorted vegetables in a mild, creamy nut-based sauce.
steps: Parboil carrots, beans, cauliflower, and peas until just tender.
Soak cashews 10 min; blend with onion, tomato, and cardamom to a smooth paste.
Heat butter and cook the paste 8-10 min. Add garam masala and bay leaf.
Add 1 cup water and simmered vegetables; cook 10 min.
Stir in cream and paneer; simmer 5 min and serve.

name: Sambar
description: South Indian lentil stew with mixed vegetables in a tangy tamarind broth.
steps: Pressure cook toor dal until mushy. Mash well.
Boil carrot and beans until tender. Add tomato and onion.
Mix in tamarind paste, sambar powder, turmeric, and salt with the vegetables. Simmer 10 min.
Add mashed dal; stir to combine. Simmer 10 min.
Make tadka: heat coconut oil, splutter mustard seeds, curry leaves, and dried red chili.
Pour tadka over sambar and serve with idli or rice.

name: Aloo Palak
description: Soft potatoes and spinach in a lightly spiced gravy.
steps: Prep potatoes and spinach: wash, peel if needed, and cut into bite-sized pieces.
Heat oil in a pan over medium heat. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter for 30 seconds.
Add chopped onion and sauté 5-6 min until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and green chili; cook 1 min.
Add tomatoes, turmeric, coriander powder, and salt. Cook until tomatoes break down, about 4-5 min.
Add potatoes and spinach. Stir to coat with masala. Add spinach and cook until wilted. Cover and cook on low heat 15-20 min, stirring occasionally.
Sprinkle garam masala, adjust salt, garnish with cilantro and serve hot with rice or roti.

name: Sri Lankan Beetroot Curry
description: Tender beetroot in a fragrant coconut-spiced gravy.
steps: Peel and cube beetroot. Simmer in salted water until just tender, 15 min. Drain.
Heat coconut oil; splutter mustard seeds and curry leaves. Add onion and sauté 4 min.
Add garlic, chili, and tomato. Cook 3 min. Add turmeric and salt.
Add beetroot and coconut milk. Simmer 10 min until gravy thickens.
Serve warm with rice or roti.

name: Begun Bhaja
description: Pan-fried eggplant slices seasoned with turmeric and mustard oil.
steps: Slice eggplant into 1/2-inch rounds. Sprinkle with salt and let sit 10 min; pat dry.
Mix turmeric, chili powder, and salt; rub on both sides of eggplant slices.
Heat mustard oil until smoking; fry eggplant rounds 3-4 min per side until golden.
Drain on paper towel. Fry thin onion rings until crispy.
Serve eggplant topped with crispy onion rings as a side.

name: Lauki Chana Dal
description: Bottle gourd and split chickpea dal in a light aromatic broth.
steps: Rinse chana dal thoroughly and soak 30 min. Pressure cook with 3 cups water for 3 whistles until soft.
Heat oil in a pan. Add cumin seeds, then onion; sauté until golden, about 5 min.
Add ginger-garlic paste, green chili, turmeric, and cook 1 min. Add tomatoes and cook 4 min.
Pour the cooked lentils into the masala. Stir well and simmer 10 min.
Make a tadka: heat ghee in a small pan, add dried red chili and cumin; pour over dal.
Adjust salt and consistency with water. Serve with steamed rice.

name: Karela Sabzi
description: Bitter gourd dry-stir-fry with caramelized onion and spices.
steps: Slice bitter gourd thinly; rub with salt and turmeric, rest 20 min; squeeze and rinse to reduce bitterness.
Heat oil and fry bitter gourd until crispy edges form, about 8-10 min.
Add onion and cook until golden, 5 min. Add tomato and all spices.
Cook 5 min until everything comes together. Adjust salt and sugar balance.
Serve as a side dish with dal and rice.

name: Cabbage Poriyal
description: South Indian-style stir-fried cabbage and carrot with coconut and mustard seeds.
steps: Shred cabbage finely; grate carrot. Chop onion small.
Heat coconut oil; splutter mustard seeds. Add curry leaves and dried red chili.
Add onion and sauté 3 min. Add carrot; cook 3 min.
Add cabbage, turmeric, and salt; stir-fry on high heat 5-6 min.
Sprinkle grated coconut, toss once, and serve.

name: Dal Bhat
description: Nepal's beloved everyday meal — creamy lentil dal served with steamed rice and vegetable sides.
steps: Rinse red lentils thoroughly and soak 30 min. Pressure cook with 3 cups water for 3 whistles until soft.
Heat oil in a pan. Add cumin seeds, then onion; sauté until golden, about 5 min.
Add ginger-garlic paste, green chili, turmeric, and cook 1 min. Add tomatoes and cook 4 min.
Pour the cooked lentils into the masala. Stir well and simmer 10 min.
Make a tadka: heat ghee in a small pan, add dried red chili and cumin; pour over dal.
Adjust salt and consistency with water. Serve with steamed rice.

name: Gundruk ko Jhol
description: Fermented leafy greens soup — a tangy, nourishing Nepali classic.
steps: Soak gundruk in warm water 15 min to rehydrate; squeeze and chop roughly. Wash fresh greens.
Heat mustard oil; add onion and sauté 4 min. Add garlic, ginger, and turmeric; cook 1 min.
Add tomato and cook until soft, 4 min. Add the gundruk and fresh greens.
Pour in 2 cups water; simmer 15 min until greens are fully tender.
Crush timur and stir in. Adjust salt and serve with dhido or rice.

name: Alu Tama
description: Potato and bamboo shoot curry with black-eyed peas — a quintessential Nepali dish.
steps: Soak black-eyed peas overnight; boil until almost tender. Rinse bamboo shoots if canned.
Heat mustard oil; add onion and sauté 5 min until golden.
Add garlic, ginger, chili, turmeric, and coriander; cook 1 min.
Add tomatoes; cook 4 min. Add potatoes, bamboo shoots, and black-eyed peas.
Add 1.5 cups water; cover and cook 20 min until potato is tender.
Stir in crushed timur, adjust salt, and serve with rice.

name: Kwati
description: Mixed sprouted bean soup — served during Janai Purnima festival in Nepal.
steps: Soak the bean mix overnight; sprout for 1-2 days for best nutrition, or boil directly until tender.
Pressure cook beans 4 whistles. Rinse bamboo shoots.
Heat oil; sauté onion 5 min. Add garlic, ginger, chili, and turmeric; cook 1 min.
Add tomatoes; cook 4 min. Add cooked beans and bamboo shoots.
Add 2 cups broth or water; simmer 20 min. Stir in timur.
Adjust salt, garnish with cilantro, and serve.

name: Rayo ko Saag
description: Stir-fried mustard greens seasoned with timur and mustard oil — simple and nutritious.
steps: Wash mustard greens thoroughly. Blanch in boiling water 2 min, drain, and roughly chop.
Heat oil or ghee in a pan. Add fenugreek seeds or cumin and let splutter.
Add onion and sauté until soft, 4-5 min. Add ginger-garlic paste and green chili; cook 1 min.
Add tomato, turmeric, and salt. Cook until tomato softens, about 4 min.
Add the chopped mustard greens and mix well. Cook on medium heat 8-10 min, stirring occasionally.
Finish with a squeeze of lemon and serve hot with dhido or rice.

name: Methi Aloo
description: Fresh fenugreek leaves cooked with potato in a lightly spiced Nepali style.
steps: Wash and roughly chop fenugreek leaves. Peel and cube potatoes.
Heat mustard oil; add onion and sauté 4 min. Add garlic and chili; cook 1 min.
Add tomato, turmeric, coriander, and salt; cook 3 min.
Add potatoes; stir to coat. Cover and cook 12 min.
Add fenugreek leaves; toss and cook uncovered 5 min until leaves wilt and potato is tender.

name: Aloo Bodi
description: Potato and black-eyed peas curry — a hearty everyday Nepali tarkari.
steps: Soak black-eyed peas overnight; boil until tender, about 20 min.
Heat oil; sauté onion 5 min. Add ginger-garlic and chili; cook 1 min.
Add tomato, turmeric, coriander; cook 4 min. Add potato and drained black-eyed peas.
Add 1 cup water; cover and simmer 20 min until potato is soft.
Stir in timur, adjust salt, serve with rice.

name: Saag ko Tarkari
description: A nutritious mix of three greens — spinach, mustard, and fenugreek — Nepali style.
steps: Wash mixed greens (spinach, mustard, fenugreek) thoroughly. Blanch in boiling water 2 min, drain, and roughly chop.
Heat oil or ghee in a pan. Add fenugreek seeds or cumin and let splutter.
Add onion and sauté until soft, 4-5 min. Add ginger-garlic paste and green chili; cook 1 min.
Add tomato, turmeric, and salt. Cook until tomato softens, about 4 min.
Add the chopped mixed greens (spinach, mustard, fenugreek) and mix well. Cook on medium heat 8-10 min, stirring occasionally.
Finish with a squeeze of lemon and serve hot with dhido or rice.

name: Nepali Kauli Tarkari
description: Cauliflower and potato simmered in timur-spiced Nepali gravy.
steps: Prep cauliflower and potato: wash, peel if needed, and cut into bite-sized pieces.
Heat oil in a pan over medium heat. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter for 30 seconds.
Add chopped onion and sauté 5-6 min until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and green chili; cook 1 min.
Add tomatoes, turmeric, coriander powder, and salt. Cook until tomatoes break down, about 4-5 min.
Add cauliflower and potato. Stir to coat with masala. Stir in timur at the end. Cover and cook on low heat 15-20 min, stirring occasionally.
Sprinkle garam masala, adjust salt, garnish with cilantro and serve hot with rice or roti.

name: Bodi ko Tarkari
description: Black-eyed peas simmered in a timur-spiced tomato gravy.
steps: Prep black-eyed peas: wash, peel if needed, and cut into bite-sized pieces.
Heat oil in a pan over medium heat. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter for 30 seconds.
Add chopped onion and sauté 5-6 min until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and green chili; cook 1 min.
Add tomatoes, turmeric, coriander powder, and salt. Cook until tomatoes break down, about 4-5 min.
Add black-eyed peas. Stir to coat with masala. Add 1 cup water and simmer 15 min. Cover and cook on low heat 15-20 min, stirring occasionally.
Sprinkle garam masala, adjust salt, garnish with cilantro and serve hot with rice or roti.

name: Tama Bodi
description: Bamboo shoots and black-eyed peas curry with potato — a Newari favourite.
steps: Prep potato, bamboo shoots, and black-eyed peas: wash, peel if needed, and cut into bite-sized pieces.
Heat oil in a pan over medium heat. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter for 30 seconds.
Add chopped onion and sauté 5-6 min until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and green chili; cook 1 min.
Add tomatoes, turmeric, coriander powder, and salt. Cook until tomatoes break down, about 4-5 min.
Add potato, bamboo shoots, and black-eyed peas. Stir to coat with masala. Add 1.5 cups water; simmer 20 min. Cover and cook on low heat 15-20 min, stirring occasionally.
Sprinkle garam masala, adjust salt, garnish with cilantro and serve hot with rice or roti.

name: Bok Choy Tarkari
description: Tender bok choy stir-fried in a light garlic-tomato sauce, Nepali style.
steps: Wash and halve bok choy lengthwise. Separate leaves and stems.
Heat oil; fry garlic 30 sec until fragrant. Add onion and sauté 3 min.
Add tomato, turmeric, and salt; cook 2 min.
Add bok choy stems first; stir-fry 2 min, then add leaves.
Drizzle soy sauce, stir in timur, cook 2 min. Serve immediately.

name: Rayo Aloo
description: Mustard greens with potato in mustard oil — a simple Nepali winter dish.
steps: Wash and chop mustard greens. Peel and cube potatoes.
Heat mustard oil; sauté onion and garlic 4 min. Add chili and turmeric.
Add potato and stir-fry 3 min. Add tomato and salt; cook 3 min.
Add mustard greens; toss well. Cover and cook 12 min on low heat.
Uncover, stir, and cook 2 more min. Serve with rice.

name: Mula ko Tarkari
description: Radish and potato curry simmered in a fragrant Nepali masala.
steps: Prep radish and potato: wash, peel if needed, and cut into bite-sized pieces.
Heat oil in a pan over medium heat. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter for 30 seconds.
Add chopped onion and sauté 5-6 min until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and green chili; cook 1 min.
Add tomatoes, turmeric, coriander powder, and salt. Cook until tomatoes break down, about 4-5 min.
Add radish and potato. Stir to coat with masala. Cover and cook on low heat 15-20 min, stirring occasionally.
Sprinkle garam masala, adjust salt, garnish with cilantro and serve hot with rice or roti.

name: Bhanta ko Jhol
description: Eggplant simmered with mustard greens in a Nepali-style broth.
steps: Quarter the eggplants; chop mustard greens roughly.
Heat mustard oil; sauté onion and garlic 4 min. Add chili and turmeric.
Add tomato; cook 3 min. Add eggplant; stir to coat.
Add 1 cup water; cover and cook 15 min until eggplant is very soft.
Add mustard greens; cook 5 min. Stir in timur and serve.

name: Bodi Mushroom
description: Black-eyed peas with mushroom in a timur-spiced gravy.
steps: Prep mushroom and black-eyed peas: wash, peel if needed, and cut into bite-sized pieces.
Heat oil in a pan over medium heat. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter for 30 seconds.
Add chopped onion and sauté 5-6 min until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and green chili; cook 1 min.
Add tomatoes, turmeric, coriander powder, and salt. Cook until tomatoes break down, about 4-5 min.
Add mushroom and black-eyed peas. Stir to coat with masala. Add 0.5 cup water and simmer 10 min. Cover and cook on low heat 15-20 min, stirring occasionally.
Sprinkle garam masala, adjust salt, garnish with cilantro and serve hot with rice or roti.

name: Methi Dal
description: Red lentil dal tempered with fresh fenugreek leaves and aromatic spices.
steps: Rinse red lentils with fenugreek thoroughly and soak 30 min. Pressure cook with 3 cups water for 3 whistles until soft.
Heat oil in a pan. Add cumin seeds, then onion; sauté until golden, about 5 min.
Add ginger-garlic paste, green chili, turmeric, and cook 1 min. Add tomatoes and cook 4 min.
Pour the cooked lentils into the masala. Stir well and simmer 10 min.
Make a tadka: heat ghee in a small pan, add dried red chili and cumin; pour over dal.
Adjust salt and consistency with water. Serve with steamed rice.

name: Palak Chana
description: Spinach and chickpea curry — high protein, high iron, all flavour.
steps: Boil chickpeas until tender. Blanch spinach and blend roughly.
Heat oil; splutter cumin. Sauté onion until golden, 5-6 min.
Add ginger-garlic, turmeric, coriander, and salt; cook 1 min.
Add tomato; cook 4 min. Add chickpeas and spinach puree.
Simmer 10 min. Sprinkle garam masala and serve.

name: Methi Chana
description: Chickpeas cooked with fresh fenugreek leaves in a tangy masala.
steps: Prep chickpeas and fenugreek leaves: wash, peel if needed, and cut into bite-sized pieces.
Heat oil in a pan over medium heat. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter for 30 seconds.
Add chopped onion and sauté 5-6 min until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and green chili; cook 1 min.
Add tomatoes, turmeric, coriander powder, and salt. Cook until tomatoes break down, about 4-5 min.
Add chickpeas and fenugreek leaves. Stir to coat with masala. Cover and cook on low heat 15-20 min, stirring occasionally.
Sprinkle garam masala, adjust salt, garnish with cilantro and serve hot with rice or roti.

name: Aloo Methi
description: Potato stir-fried with fresh fenugreek — a quick, aromatic dry sabzi.
steps: Prep potato and fenugreek leaves: wash, peel if needed, and cut into bite-sized pieces.
Heat oil in a pan over medium heat. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter for 30 seconds.
Add chopped onion and sauté 5-6 min until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and green chili; cook 1 min.
Add tomatoes, turmeric, coriander powder, and salt. Cook until tomatoes break down, about 4-5 min.
Add potato and fenugreek leaves. Stir to coat with masala. Cover and cook on low heat 15-20 min, stirring occasionally.
Sprinkle garam masala, adjust salt, garnish with cilantro and serve hot with rice or roti.

name: Paneer Bhurji
description: Scrambled paneer with onion, tomato, and bell pepper — quick and satisfying.
steps: Crumble paneer coarsely. Dice bell pepper and onion; chop tomato.
Heat butter; splutter cumin. Sauté onion until pink, 3-4 min.
Add ginger, green chili, turmeric, and bell pepper; cook 2 min.
Add tomato, coriander, and salt; cook 3 min.
Add crumbled paneer; toss gently 2-3 min. Sprinkle garam masala and serve.

name: Mushroom Paneer
description: Earthy mushrooms and paneer in a spiced bell-pepper tomato gravy.
steps: Prep mushroom and paneer: wash, peel if needed, and cut into bite-sized pieces.
Heat oil in a pan over medium heat. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter for 30 seconds.
Add chopped onion and sauté 5-6 min until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and green chili; cook 1 min.
Add tomatoes, turmeric, coriander powder, and salt. Cook until tomatoes break down, about 4-5 min.
Add mushroom and paneer. Stir to coat with masala. Cover and cook on low heat 15-20 min, stirring occasionally.
Sprinkle garam masala, adjust salt, garnish with cilantro and serve hot with rice or roti.

name: Bharwa Baingan
description: Baby eggplants stuffed with spiced peas and nut filling, then braised.
steps: Slit eggplants keeping stem; make a + cut at base. Mix peas, peanuts, and all spices for stuffing.
Stuff eggplants firmly. Heat oil and sear stuffed eggplants 3-4 min.
Add onion and tomato around eggplants; season with salt.
Cover and cook on low heat 20-25 min, turning eggplants gently halfway.
Serve hot garnished with cilantro.

name: Baingan Aloo
description: Eggplant and potato braised together in a robust spiced gravy.
steps: Prep eggplant and potato: wash, peel if needed, and cut into bite-sized pieces.
Heat oil in a pan over medium heat. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter for 30 seconds.
Add chopped onion and sauté 5-6 min until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and green chili; cook 1 min.
Add tomatoes, turmeric, coriander powder, and salt. Cook until tomatoes break down, about 4-5 min.
Add eggplant and potato. Stir to coat with masala. Cover and cook on low heat 15-20 min, stirring occasionally.
Sprinkle garam masala, adjust salt, garnish with cilantro and serve hot with rice or roti.

name: Eggplant Chickpea Curry
description: Chunky eggplant and chickpeas in a smoky masala sauce.
steps: Prep eggplant and chickpeas: wash, peel if needed, and cut into bite-sized pieces.
Heat oil in a pan over medium heat. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter for 30 seconds.
Add chopped onion and sauté 5-6 min until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and green chili; cook 1 min.
Add tomatoes, turmeric, coriander powder, and salt. Cook until tomatoes break down, about 4-5 min.
Add eggplant and chickpeas. Stir to coat with masala. Cover and cook on low heat 15-20 min, stirring occasionally.
Sprinkle garam masala, adjust salt, garnish with cilantro and serve hot with rice or roti.

name: Mushroom Palak
description: Sliced mushrooms and spinach in a light, garlic-forward gravy.
steps: Prep mushroom and spinach: wash, peel if needed, and cut into bite-sized pieces.
Heat oil in a pan over medium heat. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter for 30 seconds.
Add chopped onion and sauté 5-6 min until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and green chili; cook 1 min.
Add tomatoes, turmeric, coriander powder, and salt. Cook until tomatoes break down, about 4-5 min.
Add mushroom and spinach. Stir to coat with masala. Wilt spinach in last 5 min of cooking. Cover and cook on low heat 15-20 min, stirring occasionally.
Sprinkle garam masala, adjust salt, garnish with cilantro and serve hot with rice or roti.

name: Cauliflower Dal
description: Cauliflower florets simmered into a hearty yellow lentil dal.
steps: Rinse lentils thoroughly and soak 30 min. Pressure cook with 3 cups water for 3 whistles until soft.
Heat oil in a pan. Add cumin seeds, then onion; sauté until golden, about 5 min.
Add ginger-garlic paste, green chili, turmeric, and cook 1 min. Add tomatoes and cook 4 min.
Pour the cooked lentils into the masala. Stir well and simmer 10 min.
Make a tadka: heat ghee in a small pan, add dried red chili and cumin; pour over dal.
Adjust salt and consistency with water. Serve with steamed rice.

name: Okra Aloo Masala
description: Crispy okra and potato pieces in a dry, tangy masala coating.
steps: Par-boil potatoes until 80% done; cut into wedges. Slice okra into 1-inch pieces.
Heat oil and fry okra until crispy, 8-10 min. Remove.
In same oil fry potato wedges until golden. Remove.
Sauté onion 4 min; add tomato and all spices; cook 3 min.
Return okra and potato; toss to coat. Cook 3 min and serve.

name: Okra Mushroom
description: Okra and mushroom stir-fry with cumin and coriander.
steps: Prep okra and mushroom: wash, peel if needed, and cut into bite-sized pieces.
Heat oil in a pan over medium heat. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter for 30 seconds.
Add chopped onion and sauté 5-6 min until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and green chili; cook 1 min.
Add tomatoes, turmeric, coriander powder, and salt. Cook until tomatoes break down, about 4-5 min.
Add okra and mushroom. Stir to coat with masala. Cover and cook on low heat 15-20 min, stirring occasionally.
Sprinkle garam masala, adjust salt, garnish with cilantro and serve hot with rice or roti.

name: Okra Chickpea
description: Okra and chickpeas in a light masala — crispy and protein-rich.
steps: Prep okra and chickpeas: wash, peel if needed, and cut into bite-sized pieces.
Heat oil in a pan over medium heat. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter for 30 seconds.
Add chopped onion and sauté 5-6 min until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and green chili; cook 1 min.
Add tomatoes, turmeric, coriander powder, and salt. Cook until tomatoes break down, about 4-5 min.
Add okra and chickpeas. Stir to coat with masala. Cover and cook on low heat 15-20 min, stirring occasionally.
Sprinkle garam masala, adjust salt, garnish with cilantro and serve hot with rice or roti.

name: Okra Green Beans Sabzi
description: Okra and green beans dry stir-fry with mustard seeds.
steps: Prep okra and green beans: wash, peel if needed, and cut into bite-sized pieces.
Heat oil in a pan over medium heat. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter for 30 seconds.
Add chopped onion and sauté 5-6 min until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and green chili; cook 1 min.
Add tomatoes, turmeric, coriander powder, and salt. Cook until tomatoes break down, about 4-5 min.
Add okra and green beans. Stir to coat with masala. Cover and cook on low heat 15-20 min, stirring occasionally.
Sprinkle garam masala, adjust salt, garnish with cilantro and serve hot with rice or roti.

name: Okra Lentil Dal
description: Okra simmered into masoor dal for a thick, nutritious one-pot meal.
steps: Rinse lentils with okra thoroughly and soak 30 min. Pressure cook with 3 cups water for 3 whistles until soft.
Heat oil in a pan. Add cumin seeds, then onion; sauté until golden, about 5 min.
Add ginger-garlic paste, green chili, turmeric, and cook 1 min. Add tomatoes and cook 4 min.
Pour the cooked lentils into the masala. Stir well and simmer 10 min.
Make a tadka: heat ghee in a small pan, add dried red chili and cumin; pour over dal.
Adjust salt and consistency with water. Serve with steamed rice.

name: Bok Choy Mushroom Stir-Fry
description: Bok choy and mushroom in a ginger-soy sauce, Nepali fusion style.
steps: Halve bok choy; slice mushrooms thickly.
Heat oils; sauté garlic and ginger 30 sec. Add onion; cook 3 min.
Add mushrooms; stir-fry on high heat 3-4 min until golden.
Add tomato, turmeric, and soy sauce; cook 2 min.
Add bok choy stems; stir-fry 1 min, then add leaves. Toss 1-2 min and serve.

name: Bok Choy Paneer
description: Crispy paneer with bok choy in a light spiced sauce.
steps: Cube paneer; pan-fry until golden. Halve bok choy.
In same pan sauté onion and garlic 3 min. Add tomato, chili, and turmeric; cook 3 min.
Add bok choy stems; cook 2 min. Add leaves and paneer.
Toss gently; sprinkle garam masala. Cook 2 min and serve.

name: Bok Choy Dal
description: Bok choy leaves wilted into red lentil dal — a nutritious one-pot.
steps: Rinse red lentils, adding bok choy in the last 5 min thoroughly and soak 30 min. Pressure cook with 3 cups water for 3 whistles until soft.
Heat oil in a pan. Add cumin seeds, then onion; sauté until golden, about 5 min.
Add ginger-garlic paste, green chili, turmeric, and cook 1 min. Add tomatoes and cook 4 min.
Pour the cooked lentils into the masala. Stir well and simmer 10 min.
Make a tadka: heat ghee in a small pan, add dried red chili and cumin; pour over dal.
Adjust salt and consistency with water. Serve with steamed rice.

name: Bok Choy Aloo
description: Bok choy and potato in a simple mustard-oil tempered tarkari.
steps: Prep potato and bok choy: wash, peel if needed, and cut into bite-sized pieces.
Heat oil in a pan over medium heat. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter for 30 seconds.
Add chopped onion and sauté 5-6 min until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and green chili; cook 1 min.
Add tomatoes, turmeric, coriander powder, and salt. Cook until tomatoes break down, about 4-5 min.
Add potato and bok choy. Stir to coat with masala. Cover and cook on low heat 15-20 min, stirring occasionally.
Sprinkle garam masala, adjust salt, garnish with cilantro and serve hot with rice or roti.

name: Bok Choy Kerau
description: Bok choy and green peas stir-fried in sesame-soy glaze.
steps: Prep bok choy and peas: wash, peel if needed, and cut into bite-sized pieces.
Heat oil in a pan over medium heat. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter for 30 seconds.
Add chopped onion and sauté 5-6 min until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and green chili; cook 1 min.
Add tomatoes, turmeric, coriander powder, and salt. Cook until tomatoes break down, about 4-5 min.
Add bok choy and peas. Stir to coat with masala. Cover and cook on low heat 15-20 min, stirring occasionally.
Sprinkle garam masala, adjust salt, garnish with cilantro and serve hot with rice or roti.

name: Tama Palak

steps: Prep bamboo shoots and spinach: wash, peel if needed, and cut into bite-sized pieces.
Heat oil in a pan over medium heat. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter for 30 seconds.
Add chopped onion and sauté 5-6 min until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and green chili; cook 1 min.

Add bamboo shoots and spinach. Stir to coat with masala. Add spinach last 5 min. Cover and cook on low heat 15-20 min, stirring occasionally.
Sprinkle garam masala, adjust salt, garnish with cilantro and serve hot with rice or roti.

Heat oil in a pan. Add cumin seeds, then onion; sauté until golden, about 5 min.

name: Lauki Paneer
description: Bottle gourd and paneer cubes in a creamy tomato sauce.
steps: Prep bottle gourd and paneer: wash, peel if needed, and cut into bite-sized pieces.

name: Karela Aloo
description: Bitter gourd and potato dry stir-fry with tangy amchur.
steps: Salt and rest bitter gourd slices 20 min; rinse to reduce bitterness. Cube potatoes.
Fry bitter gourd in oil 8 min until crispy. Remove.
Sauté onion in same oil 4 min. Add potato; cook 10 min.
Add tomato and all spices; cook 3 min. Return bitter gourd.
Toss together, cook 3 min and serve.

name: Karela Mushroom
description: Bitter gourd and mushroom stir-fried with caramelized onion.
steps: Salt and rest bitter gourd 20 min; rinse and pat dry. Slice mushroom.
Fry bitter gourd until golden edges form, 8 min. Remove.
Sauté onion until golden, 5 min. Add mushroom; cook 4 min.
Add tomato, turmeric, and salt; cook 3 min.
Return bitter gourd; toss and serve.

name: Karela Dal
description: Bitter gourd pieces simmered into toor dal — balances bitterness beautifully.
steps: Rinse toor dal with bitter gourd thoroughly and soak 30 min. Pressure cook with 3 cups water for 3 whistles until soft.
Heat oil in a pan. Add cumin seeds, then onion; sauté until golden, about 5 min.
Add ginger-garlic paste, green chili, turmeric, and cook 1 min. Add tomatoes and cook 4 min.
Pour the cooked lentils into the masala. Stir well and simmer 10 min.
Make a tadka: heat ghee in a small pan, add dried red chili and cumin; pour over dal.
Adjust salt and consistency with water. Serve with steamed rice.

steps: Prep bitter gourd and green beans: wash, peel if needed, and cut into bite-sized pieces.

Add chopped onion and sauté 5-6 min until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and green chili; cook 1 min.
Add tomatoes, turmeric, coriander powder, and salt. Cook until tomatoes break down, about 4-5 min.
Add bitter gourd and green beans. Stir to coat with masala. Cover and cook on low heat 15-20 min, stirring occasionally.
Sprinkle garam masala, adjust salt, garnish with cilantro and serve hot with rice or roti.

name: Mula Mushroom

steps: Prep radish and mushroom: wash, peel if needed, and cut into bite-sized pieces.
Heat oil in a pan over medium heat. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter for 30 seconds.
Add chopped onion and sauté 5-6 min until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and green chili; cook 1 min.
Add tomatoes, turmeric, coriander powder, and salt. Cook until tomatoes break down, about 4-5 min.
Add radish and mushroom. Stir to coat with masala. Cover and cook on low heat 15-20 min, stirring occasionally.
Sprinkle garam masala, adjust salt, garnish with cilantro and serve hot with rice or roti.

name: Mula ko Jhol
description: Nepali radish soup with spinach — warm, pungent, and restorative.
steps: Peel and slice radish into half-moons. Wash spinach.
Heat mustard oil; add garlic and onion. Sauté 4 min.
Add tomato, chili, and turmeric; cook 3 min.
Add radish; pour in 2 cups water. Cover and simmer 15 min.
Add spinach and timur; cook 3 min. Serve hot.

name: Shimla Mirch Aloo
description: Bell pepper and potato dry sabzi with cumin and amchur.
steps: Prep bell pepper and potato: wash, peel if needed, and cut into bite-sized pieces.

name: Shimla Mirch Mushroom

steps: Prep bell pepper and mushroom: wash, peel if needed, and cut into bite-sized pieces.

Add chopped onion and sauté 5-6 min until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and green chili; cook 1 min.

name: Gobi Matar

steps: Prep cauliflower and peas: wash, peel if needed, and cut into bite-sized pieces.
Heat oil in a pan over medium heat. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter for 30 seconds.
Add chopped onion and sauté 5-6 min until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and green chili; cook 1 min.
Add tomatoes, turmeric, coriander powder, and salt. Cook until tomatoes break down, about 4-5 min.
Add cauliflower and peas. Stir to coat with masala. Cover and cook on low heat 15-20 min, stirring occasionally.
Sprinkle garam masala, adjust salt, garnish with cilantro and serve hot with rice or roti.

name: Sem Aloo
description: Green beans and potato dry curry with mustard seeds and curry leaves.
steps: Prep beans and potato: wash, peel if needed, and cut into bite-sized pieces.
Heat oil in a pan over medium heat. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter for 30 seconds.
Add chopped onion and sauté 5-6 min until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and green chili; cook 1 min.
Add tomatoes, turmeric, coriander powder, and salt. Cook until tomatoes break down, about 4-5 min.
Add beans and potato. Stir to coat with masala. Cover and cook on low heat 15-20 min, stirring occasionally.

steps: Prep carrot and peas: wash, peel if needed, and cut into bite-sized pieces.
Heat oil in a pan over medium heat. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter for 30 seconds.
Add chopped onion and sauté 5-6 min until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and green chili; cook 1 min.
Add tomatoes, turmeric, coriander powder, and salt. Cook until tomatoes break down, about 4-5 min.
Add carrot and peas. Stir to coat with masala. Cover and cook on low heat 15-20 min, stirring occasionally.
Sprinkle garam masala, adjust salt, garnish with cilantro and serve hot with rice or roti.

name: Apple Cinnamon Baked Oats
description: Baked like a soft cake — warm oats with caramelized apple, cinnamon, and crunchy walnuts on top.
steps: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Butter an 8x8 baking dish.
Peel and dice one apple into small cubes. Slice the second apple into thin rounds for the top.
In a bowl, mix oats, baking powder, cinnamon, and salt. In another bowl whisk egg, milk, maple syrup, melted butter, and vanilla.
Scatter the diced apple over the bottom of the baking dish. Pour the oat mixture on top and stir gently to combine.
Arrange apple slices on top and scatter walnuts. Drizzle with a little extra maple syrup.
Bake for 35-40 minutes until the top is golden and the center is set. Let cool 5 minutes before scooping.

name: Chocolate Peanut Butter Overnight Oats
description: Tastes like dessert but is genuinely healthy — rich cocoa oats with peanut butter swirled in, ready when you wake up.
steps: In a jar or container, combine oats, milk, Greek yogurt, cocoa powder, honey, chia seeds, and vanilla. Stir well.
Drop in 1 tablespoon of peanut butter and swirl it through with a knife — do not fully mix so you get ribbons of peanut butter.
Seal the jar and refrigerate overnight (or at least 6 hours).
In the morning, stir and check consistency. Add a splash of milk if too thick.
Top with sliced banana, the remaining tablespoon of peanut butter, and dark chocolate chips.
Eat cold straight from the jar.

name: Savory Miso & Mushroom Oat Porridge
description: Oats cooked in broth instead of milk, loaded with sautéed mushrooms and topped with a soft-boiled egg and sesame oil.
steps: Soft-boil the egg: lower into simmering water for exactly 7 minutes, then transfer to ice water. Peel when cool.
Slice mushrooms and mince garlic. Heat olive oil in a pan over medium-high, sauté mushrooms for 5-6 minutes until golden. Add garlic for 1 minute, then soy sauce. Set aside.
Bring vegetable broth to a boil. Stir in oats and cook for 5 minutes, stirring often, until thick and creamy.
Remove from heat. Whisk miso paste into 2 tablespoons of hot water to dissolve, then stir into the oats.
Stir spinach into the porridge — it will wilt from the heat.
Pour into a bowl. Top with mushrooms, halved soft-boiled egg, sliced spring onion, sesame seeds, and a drizzle of sesame oil.

name: Avocado Toast with Poached Egg
description: Creamy smashed avocado on thick toast with a perfectly runny poached egg, chili flakes, and lemon.
steps: Toast the bread until golden and sturdy enough to hold toppings.
Halve the avocado, remove the pit, and scoop into a bowl. Add lemon juice, salt, and pepper. Smash with a fork to your preferred texture — leave some chunks.
Bring a small saucepan of water to a gentle simmer. Add vinegar. Crack each egg into a small cup first.
Stir the water to create a gentle swirl, then slide an egg into the center. Cook for 3 minutes for a runny yolk. Remove with a slotted spoon and pat dry.
Spread smashed avocado thickly on each slice of toast. Top with a poached egg.
Halve the cherry tomatoes and arrange around the egg. Season with chili flakes, a pinch of salt, drizzle of olive oil, and chives if using.

name: Greek Yogurt Parfait
description: Thick creamy yogurt layered with crunchy granola, fresh berries, and a drizzle of honey. Zero cooking, ready in 3 minutes.
steps: Slice strawberries and banana.
In a glass or bowl, spoon half the yogurt as the base layer.
Add a layer of granola, then a layer of mixed berries and banana slices.
Spoon the remaining yogurt on top.
Finish with the rest of the granola, remaining fruit, a drizzle of honey, and coconut flakes or almond butter if using.
Eat immediately so the granola stays crunchy — or pack layers separately and assemble at work.

name: Green Power Smoothie
description: Spinach, mango, and ginger blended smooth — bright green, sweet, and energizing without tasting like grass.
steps: Peel and roughly chop the ginger.
Add coconut water to the blender first, then spinach. Blend on low for 30 seconds until the spinach is fully broken down — no leafy chunks.
Add frozen mango, banana, ginger, lemon juice, and chia seeds.
Blend on high for 60 seconds until completely smooth. Add more coconut water if too thick.
Taste and add honey if you want it sweeter.
Pour and drink immediately for best nutrition, or keep chilled for up to 4 hours.

name: Berry & Beet Smoothie
description: Deep purple blend of beetroot, mixed berries, and yogurt — earthy, sweet, and packed with antioxidants.
steps: If using raw beetroot, wrap in foil and roast at 400°F for 45 minutes. Pre-cooked beetroot from a pack works just as well.
Dice the beetroot into rough chunks.
Add almond milk to the blender, then beetroot, frozen berries, banana, yogurt, honey, flaxseeds, and vanilla.
Blend on high for 90 seconds until silky smooth. The color should be a deep magenta.
Taste — add more honey if too earthy, more berries if too beetrooty.
Pour over ice and serve immediately.

name: Peanut Butter Banana Protein Smoothie
description: Thick, filling, and genuinely satisfying — blended frozen banana with peanut butter and oats keeps you full for hours.
steps: Freeze bananas ahead of time — peel them, break in half, freeze in a bag overnight. This makes the smoothie thick and creamy without ice.
Add milk to the blender first.
Add frozen bananas, peanut butter, oats, Greek yogurt, honey, and cinnamon.
Blend on high for 60-90 seconds until completely smooth and thick. It should be almost spoonable.
Add a splash more milk if it will not blend, but keep it thick.
Pour into a tall glass. Top with chocolate chips if using and eat with a spoon or thick straw.

name: Caprese Sandwich
description: Fresh mozzarella, ripe tomato, and basil on ciabatta with a balsamic glaze. Minimal effort, maximum flavor.
steps: Slice ciabatta rolls in half and drizzle the cut sides with olive oil. Toast in a dry pan or under the broiler for 2-3 minutes until golden.
Slice tomatoes and mozzarella into thick rounds, about 1cm each.
Season tomatoes with salt and pepper.
Layer mozzarella and tomato slices alternately on the bottom half of each roll.
Tuck basil leaves between the slices. Add arugula on top if using.
Drizzle generously with balsamic glaze, close the sandwich, press gently, and serve immediately.

name: Spiced Egg Salad Sandwich
description: Creamy egg salad with mustard, curry powder, and crunchy celery — way more interesting than the plain version.
steps: Hard-boil eggs: place in cold water, bring to a boil, cook for 10 minutes, then transfer to ice water for 5 minutes. Peel and chop into rough chunks.
Finely dice celery and slice spring onions.
In a bowl, mix mayonnaise, Dijon mustard, curry powder, lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
Add chopped eggs, celery, and spring onion. Fold gently — leave some texture, do not mash it smooth.
Toast bread if preferred. Lay lettuce on two slices.
Spoon egg salad generously on the lettuce. Top with remaining bread slices, press lightly, and cut diagonally.

name: Grilled Veggie & Pesto Sandwich
description: Charred zucchini, peppers, and eggplant layered on crusty bread with pesto and provolone.
steps: Slice zucchini and eggplant lengthwise into 0.5cm strips. Slice pepper into flat panels. Slice onion into rounds.
Brush vegetables with olive oil and season with salt and pepper.
Heat a grill pan over high heat until smoking. Grill vegetables in batches for 2-3 minutes per side until charred. Drizzle with balsamic vinegar while still warm.
Spread pesto generously on all four slices of bread.
Layer grilled vegetables on two slices. Top with sliced or torn cheese.
Press the sandwich in the grill pan for 2 minutes per side until bread is toasted and cheese melts, or serve as is.

name: Tropical Fruit Bowl with Lime & Chili
description: Mango, pineapple, papaya, and watermelon with a lime-chili-honey drizzle that makes plain fruit taste incredible.
steps: Peel and slice the mango, cutting around the pit in two cheeks then scoring and cubing.
Core and dice the pineapple. Scoop out and dice the papaya, discarding seeds. Cut watermelon into triangles or cubes.
In a small bowl whisk lime juice, honey, chili powder, and a pinch of salt.
Arrange all fruit in a wide bowl or on a plate, mixing colors.
Drizzle the lime-chili dressing over the fruit.
Scatter fresh mint leaves on top. Finish with Tajin if using and serve immediately.

name: Roasted Sweet Potato & Spinach Salad
description: Warm cubes of caramelized sweet potato over baby spinach with feta, pepitas, and a maple-dijon dressing.
steps: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Peel and cube sweet potatoes into 2cm pieces. Toss with 1.5 tbsp olive oil, salt, and pepper.
Spread on a baking sheet in a single layer. Roast for 25-30 minutes, flipping halfway, until caramelized and tender.
Slice red onion into thin half-moons. Toast pepitas in a dry pan for 2-3 minutes until they start to pop.
Whisk together remaining olive oil, maple syrup, Dijon mustard, and apple cider vinegar. Season with salt and pepper.
Place spinach in a large bowl. Add warm sweet potato and red onion on top — the warmth will slightly wilt the spinach.
Drizzle dressing over, toss gently. Crumble feta on top, scatter pepitas and dried cranberries if using.

name: Cucumber, Tomato & Herb Salad
description: Simple chopped salad with cucumber, tomato, red onion, loads of fresh herbs, and a sharp lemon-olive oil dressing.
steps: Dice cucumber into 1cm pieces. Quarter the tomatoes and remove excess seeds so the salad does not get watery.
Thinly slice red onion. To mellow the sharpness, soak in cold water for 5 minutes then drain.
Roughly chop parsley and mint — use a lot, they are not garnish here, they are the salad.
Combine cucumber, tomato, onion, parsley, and mint in a large bowl.
Whisk olive oil and lemon juice with salt and pepper. Pour over the salad and toss well.
Let sit for 5 minutes before serving so the flavors meld. Top with sumac and crumbled feta if using.
